meaning: The number one. One of the most basic and ancient characters — a single horizontal stroke.
example_en: yī gè rén — one person

meaning: The number two. Two horizontal strokes representing two units.
example_en: èr yuè — February

meaning: The number three. Three horizontal strokes.
example_en: sān tiān — three days

meaning: A person. The character looks like a walking figure with two legs spread apart.
example_en: Zhōngguó rén — Chinese person

meaning: Mouth, opening. A square representing an open mouth. Used as a counter for people.
example_en: kāi kǒu — open one's mouth

meaning: Big, large, great. A person with arms outstretched wide — showing size.
example_en: hěn dà — very big

meaning: Small, little. Three strokes showing something tiny. Opposite of 大.
example_en: xiǎo xīn — be careful

meaning: Middle, center. A line through the center of a box — hitting the middle.
example_en: zhōngjiān — in the middle

meaning: Country, nation. A jewel inside a border — something precious enclosed by boundaries.
example_en: guójiā — country, nation

meaning: Sun or day. Originally a circle with a dot for the sun. Now square with a middle line.
example_en: měi rì — every day

meaning: Moon or month. A crescent moon shape. Also used for months of the year.
example_en: yuèliang — the moon

meaning: Mountain. Three peaks rising — the middle one tallest. Very recognizable pictograph.
example_en: gāo shān — high mountain

meaning: Water. Looks like flowing water with a central stream and side ripples.
example_en: hē shuǐ — drink water

meaning: Fire. Resembles flames rising upward from a base. A very old pictograph.
example_en: dà huǒ — big fire

meaning: Wood, tree. A cross shape with roots going down and branches going up.
example_en: mùtou — wood, timber

meaning: Sky, heaven, day. A line above 大 (big person) — the sky above everything.
example_en: jīntiān — today

meaning: Ground, earth, place. Also an adverbial particle (de) in spoken Chinese.
example_en: tǔdì — land, soil

meaning: Up, above, on top. A short line above a longer line — above.
example_en: shàngmiàn — above, on top

meaning: Down, below, under. A short line below a longer line — below.
example_en: xiàmiàn — below, underneath

meaning: I, me. First person singular pronoun. Contains the radical for hand holding a weapon.
example_en: wǒ shì xuéshēng — I am a student

meaning: You (singular, informal). Second person pronoun used in everyday speech.
example_en: nǐ hǎo — hello

meaning: He, him. Third person masculine pronoun. Sounds identical to 她 (she) and 它 (it).
example_en: tā shì wǒ péngyou — he is my friend

meaning: To be (is, am, are). The main linking verb. Negated as 不是 (bú shì — is not).
example_en: wǒ shì lǎoshī — I am a teacher

meaning: To have; there is/are. Negated as 没有 (méiyǒu). One of the most used verbs.
example_en: wǒ yǒu wèntí — I have a question

meaning: Negation: not, no. Changes to 2nd tone (bú) before another 4th-tone syllable.
example_en: wǒ bù zhīdào — I don't know

meaning: Good, well, fine. A woman 女 next to a child 子 — a good family scene.
example_en: hěn hǎo — very good

meaning: To come, to arrive. Opposite of 去 (qù, to go).
example_en: tā lái le — he has come

meaning: To go, to leave for. Opposite of 来 (lái, to come).
example_en: wǒ qù xuéxiào — I go to school

meaning: To speak, say, tell. The speech radical 讠on the left shows it relates to language.
example_en: tā shuō Zhōngwén — he speaks Chinese

meaning: To look at, watch, read. A hand 手 above an eye 目 — shielding eyes to look far.
example_en: wǒ kàn shū — I read books

meaning: To eat. The mouth radical 口 on the left. 吃饭 (eat rice) means to eat a meal.
example_en: wǒ chī zǎofàn — I eat breakfast

meaning: To drink. Mouth radical 口 on the left. 喝水 = drink water; 喝茶 = drink tea.
example_en: wǒ hē chá — I drink tea

meaning: To study, learn. 学生 = student; 学校 = school. A very common character.
example_en: wǒ xué Zhōngwén — I study Chinese

meaning: Year. Used for counting years. 今年 = this year; 新年 = new year.
example_en: jīnnián — this year

meaning: Many, much, more. 多少 (duōshao) = how much/many? Very common in questions.
example_en: hěn duō rén — many people

meaning: Few, little, less. Opposite of 多 (duō). Also pronounced shào meaning young.
example_en: shǎo yīdiǎn — a little less

meaning: New. 新年 = New Year; 新闻 = news. Opposite of 旧 (jiù, old/used).
example_en: xīn shū — new book

meaning: Old, aged. 老师 (teacher) = 'old master'. 老朋友 = old friend.
example_en: lǎo péngyou — old friend

meaning: Tall, high. 高兴 (gāoxìng) = happy (lit. high spirits). 高中 = high school.
example_en: hěn gāo — very tall

meaning: Home, family, house. A pig 豕 under a roof — a household. 回家 = go home.
example_en: wǒ huí jiā le — I'm going home

meaning: China — the Middle Kingdom. 中 (middle) + 国 (country). The most important place name to know.
example_en: wǒ zài Zhōngguó xué Zhōngwén — I study Chinese in China

meaning: The Chinese language (written). 中 (China/middle) + 文 (language/writing).
example_en: nǐ huì shuō Zhōngwén ma? — can you speak Chinese?

meaning: Japan — origin of the sun. 日 (sun) + 本 (origin/root). A great mnemonic.
example_en: Rìběn hěn piàoliang — Japan is very beautiful

meaning: The Japanese language. 日 (Japan) + 文 (language/writing).
example_en: wǒ xué Rìwén — I study Japanese

meaning: Population. 人 (person) + 口 (mouth) — the number of mouths to feed. Very logical!
example_en: Zhōngguó rénkǒu hěn duō — China has a large population

meaning: Adult, grown-up. 大 (big) + 人 (person) — a big person. Also means 'Your Excellency' formally.
example_en: tā yǐjīng shì dàrén le — he is already an adult

meaning: A petty, mean-spirited person or villain. 小 (small) + 人 (person). Used in 君子和小人 (noble vs petty).
example_en: tā shì gè xiǎorén — he is a petty person

meaning: A great or tall mountain. 大 (big) + 山 (mountain). Also a famous foreigner's Chinese name.
example_en: dàshān hěn gāo — the great mountain is very tall

meaning: Landscape, scenery. 山 (mountain) + 水 (water) — the classic Chinese artistic subject.
example_en: Zhōngguó shānshuǐ hěn měi — Chinese landscapes are beautiful

meaning: Water and fire — irreconcilable opposites. Used in 水火不容 (incompatible like water and fire).
example_en: tāmen shuǐhuǒ bùróng — they are like water and fire

meaning: To go up a mountain, to climb. 上 (up/go up) + 山 (mountain). Directional verb phrase.
example_en: wǒmen qù shàng shān — we're going to climb the mountain

meaning: To come down from a mountain. 下 (down) + 山 (mountain). Opposite of 上山.
example_en: wǒmen xià shān le — we came down the mountain

meaning: Today. 今 (now/present) + 天 (day/sky). One of the first time words you'll learn.
example_en: jīntiān tiānqì hěn hǎo — the weather is great today

meaning: Every day. Repeating 天 (day) creates 'every day' — a common Chinese pattern.
example_en: wǒ tiāntiān xué Zhōngwén — I study Chinese every day

meaning: University, college. 大 (big/great) + 学 (study) — a place of great learning.
example_en: wǒ zài dàxué xuéxí — I study at university

meaning: Student. 学 (study) + 生 (life/born) — one born to study. Extremely common word.
example_en: wǒ shì xuéshēng — I am a student

meaning: A good person, kind person. 好 (good) + 人 (person). Also used sarcastically sometimes.
example_en: tā shì gè hǎorén — he is a good person

meaning: Hello! Literally 'you good'. The standard greeting in Mandarin Chinese.
example_en: nǐ hǎo, wǒ jiào Xiǎomíng — hello, my name is Xiaoming

meaning: Not good, bad. 不 (not) + 好 (good). Simple negation. Very common in daily speech.
example_en: zhège bù hǎo — this is not good

meaning: Is not, am not, are not. 不 (not) + 是 (to be). The negation of 是. Note the tone change on 不.
example_en: wǒ bú shì lǎoshī — I am not a teacher

meaning: How many, how much. 多 (many) + 少 (few) — between many and few, i.e. what number? Essential question word.
example_en: duōshao qián? — how much (money)?

meaning: Everyone, everybody. 大 (big/great) + 家 (family/home) — the big family = everyone.
example_en: dàjiā hǎo! — hello everyone!

meaning: Country, nation, state. 国 (country) + 家 (home/family) — the nation as a home. Very common.
example_en: wǒ ài wǒ de guójiā — I love my country

meaning: To go home, return home. 回 (return) + 家 (home). Said constantly in Chinese daily life.
example_en: wǒ yào huí jiā le — I'm going home now

meaning: New Year. 新 (new) + 年 (year). 新年快乐 (xīnnián kuàilè) = Happy New Year!
example_en: xīnnián kuàilè! — Happy New Year!

meaning: This year. 今 (now/present) + 年 (year). Contrast with 去年 (last year) and 明年 (next year).
example_en: jīnnián wǒ qù Zhōngguó — this year I'm going to China

meaning: Last year. 去 (go/past) + 年 (year). A very common time expression.
example_en: qùnián wǒ zài Rìběn — last year I was in Japan

meaning: To speak, talk. 说 (say) + 话 (speech/words). The complete verb phrase for speaking.
example_en: qǐng mànmàn shuōhuà — please speak slowly

meaning: Good-looking, attractive, nice to watch. 好 (good) + 看 (look). Works for people and things.
example_en: zhège diànyǐng hěn hǎokàn — this movie is great

meaning: Delicious, tasty. 好 (good) + 吃 (eat). One of the most useful words for a foodie!
example_en: zhège hěn hǎochī! — this is delicious!

meaning: Tasty to drink, delicious (for beverages). 好 (good) + 喝 (drink). Parallel to 好吃.
example_en: zhège chá hěn hǎohē — this tea is delicious

meaning: A high mountain, tall peak. 高 (tall/high) + 山 (mountain). Often used poetically.
example_en: gāoshān liúshuǐ — high mountains and flowing water (a classic idiom)

meaning: A big fire, blaze, conflagration. 大 (big) + 火 (fire). Also slang for going viral/popular.
example_en: fāshēng le dàhuǒ — a big fire broke out

meaning: Wood, timber. Also slang for a dull, slow-witted person (a blockhead). 木 (wood) + 头 (head).
example_en: zhè shì mùtou zuò de — this is made of wood

meaning: To come up, to come upstairs. 上 (up) + 来 (come). A directional complement.
example_en: qǐng shànglái — please come up

meaning: To come down, to get down. 下 (down) + 来 (come). Opposite of 上来.
example_en: qǐng xiàlái — please come down

meaning: To go up, to head up. 上 (up) + 去 (go). Similar to 上来 but away from speaker.
example_en: tā shàngqù le — he went up

meaning: To go down; to continue doing something. 下 (down) + 去 (go). Context determines meaning.
example_en: jìxù xiàqù — continue on, keep going

meaning: Many years. 多 (many) + 年 (year). 多年来 = for many years, over the years.
example_en: duōnián lái tā yīzhí nǔlì — he has worked hard for many years

meaning: A great power, major nation. 大 (big/great) + 国 (country). China, USA, etc. are 大国.
example_en: Zhōngguó shì dàguó — China is a great power

meaning: Plural suffix for pronouns: 我们=we, 你们=you all, 他们=they. Always neutral tone, unstressed.
example_en: tāmen shì hǎo péngyou — they are good friends

meaning: This (nearby). 这个=this one. Opposite of 那 (nà, that). Essential demonstrative.
example_en: zhè shì shénme — what is this?

meaning: That (far away). 那个=that one. Also means 'then/well then'.
example_en: nà shì shuí — who is that?

meaning: Most common measure word for people and objects. 一个人=one person. 三个苹果=three apples.
example_en: sān gè píngguǒ — three apples

meaning: Possessive/modifying particle. 我的书=my book. One of the most used characters in Chinese.
example_en: wǒ de shū — my book

meaning: Marks completed action or change of state. 他来了=he came. 天黑了=it got dark.
example_en: wǒ chī le — I ate (already)

meaning: Also, too, as well. 我也是=me too. Placed before the verb.
example_en: wǒ yě xǐhuān — I also like it

meaning: And, with — connects nouns. 你和我=you and me. Not used between verbs like English 'and'.
example_en: nǐ hé wǒ — you and me

meaning: Then, right away, just. 就是=exactly, that's right. 我就来=I'll be right there.
example_en: wǒ jiù lái — I'll be right there

meaning: All, both, every. 大家都来了=everyone came. 都行=both are fine.
example_en: dàjiā dōu lái le — everyone came

meaning: Very. Default intensifier before adjectives. 很好=very good. Often just links adjective to subject.
example_en: tā hěn piàoliang — she is very beautiful

meaning: Correct, right, toward. 对不对=is that right? 对着=facing toward.
example_en: nǐ shuō de duì — you are correct

meaning: Can (learned skill), will (future), meeting. 你会吗=can you? 开会=hold a meeting.
example_en: nǐ huì shuō Zhōngwén ma — can you speak Chinese?

meaning: Negation for 有 and past actions. 没有=don't have. 没去=didn't go.
example_en: wǒ méiyǒu qián — I have no money

meaning: Still, also, yet. 还在=still here. 还是=or / still is.
example_en: nǐ hái zài ma — are you still here?

meaning: Time, hour, when. 有时=sometimes. 那时=at that time. Used in many time expressions.
example_en: yǒu shíhou wǒ hěn máng — sometimes I'm very busy

meaning: For, because of. 为什么=why. 为了=in order to.
example_en: wèishénme bù lái — why aren't you coming?

meaning: Structure word. 所以=therefore. 所有=all/everything. 厕所=restroom.
example_en: suǒyǐ wǒ lái le — so I came

meaning: By, because. 所以=therefore. 以前=before. 以为=to assume.
example_en: yǐqián wǒ bú huì — I couldn't before

meaning: But, however. 但是=but. 不但...而且=not only...but also.
example_en: xiǎng qù dàn méi qián — want to go but have no money

meaning: Like, as if. 如果=if. 如何=how. 如此=like this.
example_en: rúguǒ nǐ lái — if you come

meaning: Fruit, result. 水果=fruit. 结果=result. 如果=if.
example_en: shuǐguǒ hěn hǎochī — fruit is delicious

meaning: To use. 有用=useful. 用法=usage. 不用=no need to.
example_en: zěnme yòng — how do you use it?

meaning: Child; noun suffix. 孩子=child. 桌子=table. 饺子=dumplings.
example_en: háizi hěn kě'ài — the child is cute

meaning: Heart, mind. 小心=careful. 开心=happy. 心里=in one's heart.
example_en: wǒ hěn kāixīn — I'm very happy

meaning: Open, start, turn on. 开始=begin. 开心=happy. 开门=open door.
example_en: qǐng kāi mén — please open the door

meaning: Pass, cross; experience marker. 过来=come over. 吃过=have eaten before.
example_en: wǒ qù guò Zhōngguó — I've been to China

meaning: Result particle showing degree/manner. 做得好=do well. 跑得快=run fast.
example_en: tā pǎo de hěn kuài — he runs very fast

meaning: Can, able to (circumstantial). 能来吗=can you come? Different from 会 (learned skill).
example_en: nǐ néng bāng wǒ ma — can you help me?

meaning: Wait, et cetera. 等一下=wait a moment. 等等=et cetera.
example_en: qǐng děng yīxià — please wait a moment

meaning: Only, just. 只有=only have. 只是=it's just that. 只能=can only.
example_en: wǒ zhǐ yǒu yīgè — I only have one

meaning: To know (a fact). 知道=to know. 知识=knowledge.
example_en: nǐ zhīdào ma — do you know?

meaning: Road, way, principle. 知道=know. 道理=reason/logic.
example_en: zhè tiáo lù hěn cháng — this road is very long

meaning: Self, from. 自己=oneself. 自然=natural. 自由=freedom.
example_en: zìjǐ zuò — do it yourself

meaning: Again, also, both. 又来了=here we go again. 又...又...=both...and....
example_en: tā yòu lái le — he came again

meaning: Walk, leave. 走吧=let's go. 走路=walk. 我走了=I'm leaving.
example_en: wǒmen zǒu ba — let's go

meaning: Direction, square, method. 地方=place. 方法=method. 东方=east.
example_en: zhège fāngfǎ hěn hǎo — this method is very good

meaning: Door, gate, entrance. 开门=open door. 门口=doorway. 专门=specially.
example_en: qǐng kāi mén — please open the door

meaning: Inside, within. 里面=inside. 心里=in one's heart. 这里=here.
example_en: zài lǐmiàn — inside

meaning: Face, surface, side, noodles. 上面=above. 下面=below. 面对=face up to.
example_en: shàngmiàn yǒu shénme — what's on top?

meaning: We, us. 我 (I) + 们 (plural). 我们的=our.
example_en: wǒmen shì péngyou — we are friends

meaning: You all. 你 (you) + 们 (plural). Used for groups.
example_en: nǐmen hǎo — hello everyone

meaning: They/them. 他 (he) + 们 (plural). Mixed or male group.
example_en: tāmen lái le — they came

meaning: This one. 这 (this) + 个 (measure word). Most common demonstrative.
example_en: zhège hǎo — this one is good

meaning: That one. 那 (that) + 个 (measure word).
example_en: nàge shì shénme — what is that?

meaning: When, at the time of. 吃饭的时候=when eating.
example_en: shuìjiào de shíhou — when sleeping

meaning: Why. 为 (because of) + 什么 (what). Essential question word.
example_en: wèishénme bù lái — why aren't you coming?

meaning: Therefore, so. 因为...所以...=because...so....
example_en: suǒyǐ wǒ lái le — so I came

meaning: If. Most common conditional in Chinese.
example_en: rúguǒ nǐ lái — if you come

meaning: Fruit. 水 (water) + 果 (fruit/result). Generic for all fruits.
example_en: wǒ xǐhuān chī shuǐguǒ — I like eating fruit

meaning: Useful. 有 (have) + 用 (use). 没用=useless.
example_en: zhè hěn yǒuyòng — this is very useful

meaning: Happy, glad. 开 (open) + 心 (heart) — open-hearted. 不开心=unhappy.
example_en: wǒ hěn kāixīn — I'm very happy

meaning: To know a fact. 知 (know) + 道 (way/truth). 我知道=I know. 不知道=don't know.
example_en: nǐ zhīdào ma — do you know?

meaning: Oneself. 自 (self) + 己 (self). 自己来=come yourself.
example_en: zìjǐ zuòfàn — cook for yourself

meaning: To walk. 走 (walk) + 路 (road).
example_en: wǒ xǐhuān zǒulù — I like walking

meaning: Open the door. 开 (open) + 门 (door).
example_en: qǐng kāimén — please open the door

meaning: Inside, within. 里 (inside) + 面 (surface).
example_en: zài lǐmiàn děng wǒ — wait for me inside

meaning: Above, on top. 上 (up) + 面 (surface).
example_en: shū zài shàngmiàn — the book is on top

meaning: Below, underneath. 下 (down) + 面 (surface).
example_en: qǐng kàn xiàmiàn — please look below

meaning: Don't have, there isn't. 没 (not) + 有 (have). Most common negation of 有.
example_en: wǒ méiyǒu shíjiān — I don't have time

meaning: Time. 时 (time) + 间 (between/gap). 没时间=no time. 有时间=have time.
example_en: méiyǒu shíjiān — no time

meaning: Before, in the past. 以 (by/at) + 前 (front/before).
example_en: yǐqián wǒ bù zhīdào — I didn't know before

meaning: Method, approach. 方 (direction) + 法 (law/method).
example_en: zhège fāngfǎ hěn hǎo — this method is very good

meaning: But, however. 但 (but) + 是 (is). Most common contrastive connector.
example_en: wǒ xiǎng qù, dànshì méi qián — I want to go but have no money

meaning: Come over to this side. 过 (cross) + 来 (come). Directional complement.
example_en: nǐ guòlai yīxià — come over here

meaning: Ability, capability. 能 (can) + 力 (strength).
example_en: tā hěn yǒu nénglì — he is very capable

meaning: Only have, only when. 只 (only) + 有 (have).
example_en: zhǐyǒu yīgè fāngfǎ — there's only one way

meaning: That's right! By the way (interjection).
example_en: duì le, wǒ wàng le — oh right, I forgot

meaning: Begin, start. 开 (open) + 始 (begin). 开始了=it has begun.
example_en: wǒmen kāishǐ ba — let's begin

meaning: Wait a moment. 等 (wait) + 一 (one) + 下 (moment). Very common request.
example_en: qǐng děng yīxià — please wait a moment

meaning: Or (in questions); still is. 还 + 是. 你去还是他去=you or him?
example_en: nǐ qù háishi tā qù — are you going or is he?

meaning: Not right. 不 (not) + 对 (correct). 这样不对=this isn't right.
example_en: zhèyàng bú duì — this way is not right

meaning: Natural, naturally. 自 (self) + 然 (so/thus). 大自然=nature.
example_en: zhè hěn zìrán — this is very natural

meaning: To face, confront. 面 (face) + 对 (toward).
example_en: miànduì kùnnán — face difficulties

meaning: Usage, how to use something. 用 (use) + 法 (method).
example_en: zhège cí de yòngfǎ — the usage of this word

meaning: To think/assume (often wrongly). 以 + 为. 我以为他来了=I thought he came.
example_en: wǒ yǐwéi tā lái le — I thought he came

meaning: Walk over here. 走 (walk) + 过 (cross) + 来 (come).
example_en: tā zǒu guòlai le — he walked over

meaning: Both...and.... Pattern: 又大又漂亮=both big and beautiful.
example_en: yòu kuài yòu hǎo — both fast and good

english: in one's heart / in one's mind
meaning: In one's heart/mind. 心 (heart) + 里 (inside).
example_en: xīnlǐ hěn kāixīn — feel happy inside

meaning: Doorway, entrance. 门 (door) + 口 (mouth/opening).
example_en: zài ménkǒu děng wǒ — wait for me at the entrance
